data.title: Economist: Bitcoin's orderly decline did not cause market panic, and the drop to $79,000 was not a crash
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Anthony Pompliano, founder and CEO of Professional Capital Management, wrote on the X platform that the community has long been prepared for the scenario of Bitcoin falling to $82,000. Peter Schiff, an economist who is not friendly to Bitcoin, responded: "This time Bitcoin has fallen in a fairly orderly manner, without causing panic, and I don't think that a drop to $79,000 means a collapse."

data.title: UK Government Proposes Crime And Policing Bill To Tackle Crypto-Related Crimes
data.description:
According to ShibDaily, the UK government has introduced the Crime and Policing Bill, a legislative proposal designed to enhance law enforcement's ability to seize illicit assets, including cryptocurrencies linked to criminal activities. The bill aims to bolster financial crime enforcement as digital assets increasingly feature in illegal transactions. Currently under its second reading in the House of Commons, the proposed legislation would extend the Crown Court's authority over confiscation orders if passed.

The Crime and Policing Bill also sets guidelines for valuing and managing unsellable crypto property, ensuring a systematic approach to digital asset forfeiture. A corresponding factsheet highlights that the bill will enhance the criminal justice system's capacity to target individuals benefiting from criminal activities. This proposal builds on the Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act of 2023, which empowered authorities to freeze and confiscate cryptocurrencies tied to illegal activities. In addition to enhancing seizure powers, the bill introduces measures to streamline asset recovery, allowing law enforcement to retrieve stolen funds more efficiently and redirect them to victims. It seeks to prevent individuals involved in digital asset-related crimes from profiting from illicit activities, reinforcing efforts to curb financial misconduct in the evolving crypto landscape.

The Crime and Policing Bill is part of the UK's ongoing efforts to strengthen digital asset regulation, as authorities increase oversight of the crypto sector. Over the past year, the government has implemented stricter measures to address financial risks associated with cryptocurrencies. In October 2024, the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) reaffirmed its commitment to stringent cryptocurrency regulations, emphasizing that rigorous oversight is crucial for combating money laundering and financial crimes. The regulatory body stressed that maintaining high standards is necessary to protect investors and prevent illicit activities within the digital asset sector.

The push for stricter crypto regulations comes amid a rise in crypto-related crimes across the UK, with illicit transactions increasingly linked to the country. A particularly concerning trend involves violent home invasions targeting individuals with digital assets. In one notable case, seven British men were sentenced to a combined 76 years in prison in January after kidnapping and torturing a victim to coerce the transfer of cryptocurrency worth hundreds of thousands of dollars. This growing threat underscores the need for stronger safeguards and enforcement measures to combat financial crimes in the digital asset space.

data.title: Trump's Fiscal Tightening May Influence Federal Reserve's Rate Decisions
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Nir Kaissar, head of asset management firm Unison Advisors, suggests that U.S. President Donald Trump may have found a way to compel the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates through fiscal tightening.

Kaissar notes that in recent years, the impact of fiscal policy has been at least as significant as the Federal Reserve's historic measures. Currently, fiscal and monetary policies may be on the verge of switching roles. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has stated that the government aims to reduce the deficit to 3% of GDP. Achieving this would require Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to cut $1 trillion in spending. While it remains uncertain how much of this target will be met, the mere threat of spending cuts could already be dampening market sentiment and hindering economic growth.

By reducing expenditures, Trump might force monetary policy to ease in support of fiscal tightening. Whether these spending cuts lead to an economic slowdown is irrelevant. As long as these cuts, or the threat thereof, coincide with an economic slowdown, the Federal Reserve may be prompted to take action.

data.title: Trump's speech on Friday may increase market volatility, and cryptocurrencies fall as investors turn to low-volatility assets
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: US President Donald Trump will deliver a speech later on Friday, and market volatility seems to continue to increase. Griffin Ardern, head of options trading and research at crypto financial platform BloFin, said: "US domestic policies have become unstable, and the White House seems happy to take advantage of this instability. Given the challenges investors face in obtaining accurate forward-looking guidance, many people prefer to hold low-volatility assets... Traders need to liquidate positions to reduce exposure to specific assets before turning to other markets, which explains the decline in almost all asset classes, including cryptocurrencies." (Coindesk)

data.title: Robert Kiyosaki Warns Of U.S. Economic Collapse And Rising Inflation
data.description: According to Odaily, Robert Kiyosaki, the author of 'Rich Dad Poor Dad,' has issued a warning about the state of the U.S. economy. He claims that the economy is on the brink of collapse, inflation is set to surge, and the U.S. dollar is destined to fail. Kiyosaki emphasized that the real issue is not Bitcoin, but rather the fragmented monetary system and corrupt bankers in the United States. He suggests that Bitcoin, gold, and silver are the solutions to these problems. As global investors withdraw from U.S. bonds, Kiyosaki predicts a complete collapse of the dollar.

data.title: Blockchain intellectual property protection company Eason Technology completes HK$1.5 million financing, with Renying Capital participating
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Eason Technology, a blockchain intellectual property protection company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, announced the completion of a HK$1.5 million financing, with Renying Capital participating in the investment. After the transaction is completed, Renying Capital will own 15% of the company's total equity. The new funds are intended to be used to explore the deployment of blockchain solutions in copyright and intellectual property fields such as literature, music, film and television, drama, and games. (prnewswire)

data.title: Rich Dad Poor Dad author: The problem is not Bitcoin, but the broken US monetary system
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Robert Kiyosaki, author of "Rich Dad Poor Dad", once again sounded the alarm, saying that the US economy is collapsing, inflation will soar, and the US dollar is doomed. Robert Kiyosaki added: "Bitcoin crash? I want to buy it. The real problem is not Bitcoin, but the fragmented monetary system and corrupt bankers in the United States. The solution is Bitcoin, gold and silver." As global buyers withdraw from US bonds, Robert Kiyosaki predicts that the US dollar will collapse completely. (News.bitcoin)

data.title: Ethereum RWA staking network OpenZK receives early investment from Coinstore
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Ethereum's second-layer network OpenZK received early investment from Coinstore, and is committed to innovating Ethereum expansion, re-staking and RWA fields, building a faster, more efficient and more rewarding blockchain ecosystem. It is reported that the OpenZK airdrop points activity has just been launched this week, and early participants can get 10 times the airdrop points.

data.title: Core PCE Inflation Rate Expected To Slow In January
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Nick Timiraos, often referred to as the 'Fed's mouthpiece,' reported that economists converting CPI and PPI into PCE anticipate a 0.27% rise in core prices for January. 

The core prices had previously increased by 0.50% in January 2024. Due to this base effect, the 12-month core PCE inflation rate is expected to slow from 2.8% in December to 2.6%.

data.title: Tether CEO Predicts Bitcoin-Dominated Future Amid Fiat Currency Collapse
data.description:
According to Odaily, Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino expressed his views in a recent blog post, identifying himself as a Bitcoin Maximalist. Ardoino believes that all fiat currencies will eventually collapse due to their inherent flaws, leading to a financial reset characterized by hyperinflation. Despite USDT being a stablecoin with a market capitalization exceeding $140 billion, Ardoino views it as merely a transitional tool to help economies shift towards a Bitcoin-dominated future. He emphasized Bitcoin's resilience, claiming it is the only currency capable of enduring for the next 2000 years.

Furthermore, as Tether faces challenges from stringent regulations, Ardoino highlighted the company's focus on emerging markets. He also noted Tether's recent investments in Latin America, particularly in green energy, artificial intelligence, and agriculture.
